title: ‘Cheat code’: Harley Reid blows AFL away as Eagles demolish Tigers
subtitle: West Coast Eagles young star Harley Reid has taken the footy world by storm with one moment leaving fans completely stunned.
content:
It’s Harley Reid’s world and West Coast Eagles fans are revelling in it.
The young superstar produced his latest masterclass on Sunday as the Eagles found their wings and defied the pundits.
Going up against Richmond, many had predicted even further pain for the bottom of the ladder club, but it wasn’t to be.
The side, routinely dubbed a basket case, proved critics wrong as they demolished their rivals to record their first win in 239 days.
Their last taste of glory came in round 23 last year on August 20 when they took care of business against the Western Bulldogs in a nailbiting seven point win.
While it was a much cruisier result this time around, things got off to a worrying start after Richmond kicked the opening four goals.
After falling behind by 25 points, the Eagles flicked the switch and turned it into a one-sided contest as they kicked 16 goals to six to secure the 16.13 (109) to 10.10 (70) win.
And behind the onslaught and stealing the show was the number one pick.
Reid has shown flashes of why he was taken with the top pick, but on Sunday he was at his mercurial best.
In the early stages of the second term, Reid got the ball in the middle of the ground as he danced his way out of a contest before turning on the jets.
He burned past Tigers ruckman Toby Nankervis before sending it deep inside forward 50 and setting up another Eagles goal.
But it was his constant work around contests that had fans in disbelief. The 18-year-old, in only his fifth game, showed time and again that he’s an impossible man to tackle.
Reid shrugged off countless would be Tigers players while delivering fend offs to whoever dared try wrap their arms around him. Even Dustin Martin found himself on the receiving end.
The image, uploaded to the AFL’s social media accounts, quickly went viral.
Footy fans watching on were full of praise for the supremely talented youngster.
3AW Football wrote: “It’s Harley Reid’s world now.”
One user wrote: “Harley Reid is box office stuff. Sheesh.”
A second added: “Harley Reid is a genuine cheat code.”
While it was Reid stealing the limelight with 27 disposals, 7 clearances and a goal, several of his teammates produced stunning performances in the match.
Tim Kelly lead the way with 29 disposals while Elliot Yeo continued to turn back the clock as he racked up 27 touches, 15 clearances and two goals.
In front of goal it was the Jake Waterman show as he hauled in 12 marks and slotted six goals.
The win lifts the Eagles into 16th spot on the ladder, leaving Hawthorn and North Melbourne as the last remaining teams yet to secure a win in 2024.
The Eagles will take on Fremantle in round 6 in what is now looking like a potentially blockbuster Derby between the two WA clubs.
en_title: ‘Cheat code’: Harley Reid blows AFL away as Eagles demolish Tigers
en_content:
It’s Harley Reid’s world and West Coast Eagles fans are revelling in it.
The young superstar produced his latest masterclass on Sunday as the Eagles found their wings and defied the pundits.
Going up against Richmond, many had predicted even further pain for the bottom of the ladder club, but it wasn’t to be.
The side, routinely dubbed a basket case, proved critics wrong as they demolished their rivals to record their first win in 239 days.
Their last taste of glory came in round 23 last year on August 20 when they took care of business against the Western Bulldogs in a nailbiting seven point win.
While it was a much cruisier result this time around, things got off to a worrying start after Richmond kicked the opening four goals.
After falling behind by 25 points, the Eagles flicked the switch and turned it into a one-sided contest as they kicked 16 goals to six to secure the 16.13 (109) to 10.10 (70) win.
And behind the onslaught and stealing the show was the number one pick.
Reid has shown flashes of why he was taken with the top pick, but on Sunday he was at his mercurial best.
In the early stages of the second term, Reid got the ball in the middle of the ground as he danced his way out of a contest before turning on the jets.
He burned past Tigers ruckman Toby Nankervis before sending it deep inside forward 50 and setting up another Eagles goal.
But it was his constant work around contests that had fans in disbelief. The 18-year-old, in only his fifth game, showed time and again that he’s an impossible man to tackle.
Reid shrugged off countless would be Tigers players while delivering fend offs to whoever dared try wrap their arms around him. Even Dustin Martin found himself on the receiving end.
The image, uploaded to the AFL’s social media accounts, quickly went viral.
Footy fans watching on were full of praise for the supremely talented youngster.
3AW Football wrote: “It’s Harley Reid’s world now.”
One user wrote: “Harley Reid is box office stuff. Sheesh.”
A second added: “Harley Reid is a genuine cheat code.”
While it was Reid stealing the limelight with 27 disposals, 7 clearances and a goal, several of his teammates produced stunning performances in the match.
Tim Kelly lead the way with 29 disposals while Elliot Yeo continued to turn back the clock as he racked up 27 touches, 15 clearances and two goals.
In front of goal it was the Jake Waterman show as he hauled in 12 marks and slotted six goals.
The win lifts the Eagles into 16th spot on the ladder, leaving Hawthorn and North Melbourne as the last remaining teams yet to secure a win in 2024.
The Eagles will take on Fremantle in round 6 in what is now looking like a potentially blockbuster Derby between the two WA clubs.
en_subtitle: West Coast Eagles young star Harley Reid has taken the footy world by storm with one moment leaving fans completely stunned.

title: Security guard killed in Bondi Junction mass stabbing named as refugee Faraz Tahir
subtitle: The 30-year-old security guard, and former refugee who was killed in the Bondi Junction stabbing has been named as Faraz Tahir.
content:
A 30-year-old former refugee who was working as a security guard at Bondi Junction Westfield has been identified as the sole male victim who was killed in Saturday’s horrific stabbing attack. 
Faraz Tahir, 30, arrived in Australia about a year ago from Sri Lanka via the UNHCR from Pakistan, where his parents still live. 
A statement from the Ahmadiyya Muslim Community of Australia said Mr Tahir was known for his “unwavering dedication and kindness”. 
Mr Tahir was part of the Ahmadiyya community, which is one of the 73 sects of Islam, however Ahmadis face persecution for their religion in Muslim-majority countries like Algeria, Malaysia and Pakistan. 
Under the Pakistani constitution, the community are declared non-Muslims, and have been subjected to violent attacks, and are not allowed to vote due to their religious beliefs.
“Faraz was not only a valued member of the Ahmadiyya Muslim Community of Australia but also actively contributed to the charitable endeavours of the Ahmadiyya Muslim Youth organisation,” they said.
“Our thoughts and prayers are with Faraz’s family and loved ones during this difficult time, as well as with all the other victims and their families affected by this senseless act of violence.
“We extend our deepest condolences and stand in solidarity with the broader community as we come together to support one another in the face of tragedy.
“We commend the swift response of the authorities and offer our full co-operation with the ongoing investigation. The Ahmadiyya Muslim Community of Australia remains steadfast in its commitment to promoting peace, unity, and understanding in our society.”
Including Mr Tahir, three out of six victims have been identified since Saturday’s tragic events, including Dawn Singleton, the 25-year-old daughter of Sydney businessman John Singleton, and Sydney architect Jade Young.
First time mum Ash Good died in hospital on Saturday night, after she called on bystanders to protect her nine-month-old daughter. 
The baby girl is currently in a critical but stable condition at an ICU unit at Sydney Children’s Hospital.

title: Health department, ABS show fall in Australia’s recorded Covid deaths in 2024
subtitle: A clear trend is emerging in Covid deaths and 2024 could mark a significant milestone in the progression of the virus, new data has revealed.
content:
After years of fear and lockdowns, 2024 could mark the end of Covid’s long hold over Australia. 
Data from the Department of Health and Aged care shows a clear and steady decline in Covid associated deaths in the first four months of the year, with seven-day rolling averages slipping from about 10 in early January to below five by early April.
Since early March, the number of average deaths has stayed consistently below 5, the lowest number since the outbreak of the respiratory virus in March 2020.
The data, which comes from the National Notifiable Diseases Surveillance System, records deaths linked to the virus, but notes that factors such as other diseases of chronic conditions may also have contributed to or caused deaths associated with Covid.
The latest data from the Australian Bureau of Statistics on Covid mortality, released in late February, also suggests that while the virus is still a lethal threat, its lethality may be declining.
In January 2022, the ABS recorded 1646 deaths due to Covid in the month, while in January 2023 the figure was 753. 
In January 2024, the number was 189, though the ABS warned the figure would likely rise as additional death registrations were received by the statistics agency. 
“For every month in 2023 deaths due to Covid have been below the level of the comparable month in 2022,” the ABS said.
The health department has cautioned its seven-day rolling averages should be read with some caution, however, given the decline in Covid testing.
“With the reduction in Covid’s testing, attribution of deaths to Covid will significantly underestimate Covid mortality,” the department states.
Associate professor in epidemiology at Deakin University Hassan Vally said the fall in deaths suggested the overall threat of Covid had fallen since its explosion in the world in March 2020. 
“Principally it demonstrates how improved immunity, through vaccination and prior infection, improved treatments and a better understanding of the virus, has fundamentally altered the impact of Covid on society since its emergence,” he said. 
Australian National University infectious diseases expert Associate Professor Sanjaya Senanayake suggested the prevalence of the omicron variant was also a factor in the lower numbers.
“Omicron subvariants have continued to dominate the global Covid landscape for over two years,” he said.
“This means that the combination of immunity from natural infection with Omicron subvariants, in addition to vaccine-induced immunity, has had a positive impact on levels of severe Covid.
“In addition, the free early access to antivirals such as Paxlovid to people at risk of severe Covid has further put Australia in a great position to dampen severe disease from this virus.”
But the professor warned Covid was not “going anywhere”. 
“Covid is still circulating and it isn’t going anywhere,” he said.
“Like influenza, it will mutate. And like influenza, a big mutation, for example, into a new variant, which will be Pi, the next letter in the Greek alphabet, could lead to a significant outbreak of Covid.
“However, even if this occurs, we still have effective antivirals to protect people at risk.”

title: South Australian Police announce arrest in alleged William Holdback killing
subtitle: Police have delivered a major update in the brutal alleged slaying of a 21-year-old man that was captured on CCTV.
content:
A 27-year-old man has been arrested for the brutal alleged early morning killing of a young man last week. 
CCTV footage from an industrial area in inner-city Adelaide captured the final moments of 21-year-old William Holdback, who could be heard screaming for help about 1am as he is allegedly chased down the street by assailants.
The police launched a large-scale hunt after finding Mr Holdback’s body in the Welland area on Wednesday morning and on Sunday night, announced an arrest in the case.
“Shortly before his death William had met with two occupants of a BMW sedan in Musgrave Street,” the police said.
“A brief argument occurred and William fled into Barrpowell Street where he was fatally stabbed by the passenger of the vehicle.
“The driver of the vehicle drove the BMW into Barrpowell Street and collected the person who stabbed William and they left the scene.
“Detectives believe that the two suspects then travelled in the BMW north on South Road from Welland, then east along the Port River Expressway, along the full length of Salisbury Highway and into John Rice Ave.
“Late on Saturday, detectives went to the suspect’s northern suburbs home and arrested the 27-year-old man, charging him with murder.
“The BMW was also seized from the home and (police) interviewed the owner, who is also suspected of murder.
“That person’s role before, during and after the murder is now the subject of an intense and ongoing investigation by detectives who are continuing to compile evidence regarding that person’s involvement.”
One of the alleged attackers said “Get him ****. He can’t run that fast,” and “I’m going to stab you”.
The footage shows the alleged attackers then get back in a car and speed off.
One of the men can be heard saying that Mr Holdback had “smashed” all the windows in the car.
South Australian Police Detective Superintendent Des Bray said last week the police suspected the victim and offenders knew each other.
“This young man, we believe, arranged to meet somebody, in this street, at 1.30am. On that basis, we do not believe this death was committed by some random stranger,” he said.
The 27-year-old man was refused bail and will appear in the Adelaide Magistrates Court on Monday.

title: Security guard Faraz Tahir named as Bondi stabbing victim
subtitle: Faraz Tahir moved to Australia in search of refuge, and a better life. On Saturday afternoon, it was cut violently short.
content:
The only male victim to be killed in the horrific stabbing attack at Westfield Bondi Junction has been identified as Faraz Tahir. 
Mr Tahir, a security guard at the busy shopping centre, moved to Australia last year through a United Nations High Commissioner for Refugees program, fleeing persecution in his home country of Pakistan. 
His friend and fellow member of the Ahmadiyya Muslim Community, Shajar Ahmad, told 
He had worked just four or five shifts at Westfield, Mr Ahmad said. Yet, on Saturday afternoon – his first daytime shift at the centre – his new life here was cut tragically short. 
Mr Tahir was 
In a statement on Sunday afternoon, the Ahmadiyya Muslim Community remembered Mr Tahir as “a cherished member of our community and a dedicated security guard who tragically lost his life while serving the public during this attack”. 
“Faraz Tahir, aged 30, sought refuge in Australia just a year ago, fleeing persecution in his home country of Pakistan,” the statement continued. 
“He quickly became an integral part of our community, known for his unwavering dedication and kindness. Faraz was not only a valued member of the Ahmadiyya Muslim Community of Australia but also actively contributed to the charitable endeavours of the Ahmadiyya Muslim Youth organisation.
“Our thoughts and prayers are with Faraz’s family and loved ones during this difficult time, as well as with all the other victims and their families affected by this senseless act of violence. We extend our deepest condolences and stand in solidarity with the broader community as we come together to support one another in the face of tragedy.
“We commend the swift response of the authorities and offer our full co-operation with the ongoing investigation. The Ahmadiyya Muslim Community of Australia remains steadfast in its commitment to promoting peace, unity, and understanding in our society.”
The Australian Pakistani National Association (APNA) shared the devastating news with its community on Facebook. 
“With heavy hearts, we share the news of a tragic incident at a Sydney mall, where lives were lost, including a courageous Pakistani national faraz (sic) Ahmed (sic) who had moved from Brisbane for work. May Allah bless him for his dedication,” a post by the group read. 
“Let us stand together in solidarity, offering support and prayers to those grieving and affected by this heartbreaking loss.” 
The High Commissioner for Pakistan to Australia, Zahid Hafeez Chaudhri, extended his “deepest sympathies and prayers” to Mr Tahir’s family. 
“Our thoughts and prayers are with the victims and their families. The victims of yesterday’s horrific attacks included a Pakistani citizen Faraz Ahmed who lost his life while saving others … We are working with the family for reparation of his mortal remains to Pakistan. 
“Another Pakistani citizen got injured during the horrific attacks. He is hospitalised but is in a stable condition. We pray for his fast and full recovery.”

title: Young North star hospitalised as Geelong run riot in bloodbath outing
subtitle: Geelong’s dominant start to the 2024 season reached destructive heights on Sunday as they tore North Melbourne to shreds at home.
content:
Geelong has continued its undefeated start to 2024 — and its ominous recent record against North Melbourne — defeating the Kangaroos 21.13 (139) to 10.4 (64) on Sunday afternoon at GMHBA Stadium.
The Cats have now won 12 consecutive games against North Melbourne in a streak that stretches back to Round 12, 2016. 
The Kangaroos, now 0-5, have now conceded at least 100 points in all five games they’ve played thus far this season. 
Superstar forward Jeremy Cameron starred for Chris Scott’s brigade, booting six goals to go along with 21 disposals, eight marks and 11 score involvements. 
Tyson Stengle, Gryan Miers, Ollie Henry, Brad Close and Tanner Bruhn each registered eight score involvements of their own. 
Geelong’s dominant victory moves it to second on the ladder behind GWS, with the pair of clubs now the only undefeated teams remaining in 2024. 
Geelong opted to rest Tom Hawkins and didn’t rush Patrick Dangerfield’s return — and it turns out they didn’t need their star veterans as they ran out 75-point winners. 
Without Hawkins in attack, Jeremy Cameron had a day out as he racked up a whopping 15 disposals, four goals and eight score involvements in the first half alone. 
“It’s already a day out,” Fox Footy’s Brad Johnson said of Cameron’s work rate at half time.
“That’s what sets him apart from everyone else in the competition. He runs (the Roos) into the ground.
“North Melbourne are on their haunches because of how powerful he is.”
Cameron Mooney described Cameron’s first half as “phenomenal”. 
“With (Hawkins) out, was he going to anchor himself to the goal square? The answer is no,” he said. 
“He’s playing his natural game, getting up the ground and running his opponent into the ground.
“We’ve seen it a couple of times now when North defenders just couldn’t run back with him.
“He’s run into an open goal on a couple of occasions.”
Cameron finished his afternoon with a stuffed stat sheet: 21 disposals, six goals and 11 score involvements. 
Brandan Parfitt was another to shine in a star’s absence as he took over in the midfield without Dangerfield.
Johnson said the looming selection headache was a good one for coach Chris Scott.
“The competition for spots at Geelong is going to become tight very quickly,” he said.
“That’s where it’s going to be tough for Parfitt, (Jack) Bowes… you just can’t play them all, unfortunately.”
Mooney said he was pleased to see plenty of talent coming through at the club.
“It’s taken 12 months for (their recruits) to settle into this team and this town, and we are just seeing the fruits of it in the first five weeks of this season.”
Geelong is now one of just two teams unbeaten this season, joining GWS at the top of the table.
North Melbourne young gun Colby McKercher lasted less than a quarter at the Cattery on Sunday after a heavy collision with Geelong star Jeremy Cameron.
McKercher had attacked the footy at half-back when he made contact with Cameron coming in the other direction.
While Cameron came away rubbing his hip, McKercher made his way to the bench and later was taken to hospital as a precaution.
Fox Footy’s Cameron Mooney described the collision as a fair bump.
“It really was a flush hit from Jeremy Cameron,” he said. 
“Two boys attacking the ball, (Cameron) turned his body at the right time and got his backside right into the ribcage.”
Commentator Dwayne Russell questioned if the turning of the body was the way to go given recent Match Review scrutiny.
“The turning of the body in contests is something that’s been discouraged a bit because you don’t want to put your backside into someone else’s head,” he said.
“To lead in front on.”
“It’s how we were all taught as kids,” Mooney responded.
“Does the AFL want to discourage the turning of your backside and go in head-first rather is the way they want you to go?” Russell questioned. 
Colby McKercher was subbed out of the game following this first quarter collision with Jeremy Cameron.
North Melbourne was given AFL assistance at the end of last season after another luckless year. 
But even with that help, the Roos remain winless in 2024 – with fears their year could be just as long as the last.
North started 2023 with back-to-back wins in promising signs under new coach Alastair Clarkson.
But Clarkson and the Roos haven’t been able to replicate that start.
“Tough year for the Kangaroos who are yet to have a win this season,” commentator Dwayne Russell said on Fox Footy.
To fully appreciate the chasm between these sides, consider that Bailey Scott has played more AFL games than Brad Close but after today Close will have played in 48 more wins.
“At least they started well last year (with two straight wins). It’s been a tough time.
“Guys like Eddie Ford playing today - 26 games for only one win … makes it tough for development.”
Three-time premiership winner Cameron Mooney forecast another “tough year” at Arden Street.
“It’s going to be a tough year as a key forward for North Melbourne.”
Debutant Tyler Sellers didn’t have a touch until late in the final term as he struggled to get near it, making just one tackle in three quarters of footy. 
Originally published as

While Cameron came away rubbing his hip, McKercher made his way to the bench and later was taken to hospital as a precaution.
Fox Footy’s Cameron Mooney described the collision as a fair bump.
“It really was a flush hit from Jeremy Cameron,” he said. 
“Two boys attacking the ball, (Cameron) turned his body at the right time and got his backside right into the ribcage.”
Commentator Dwayne Russell questioned if the turning of the body was the way to go given recent Match Review scrutiny.
“The turning of the body in contests is something that’s been discouraged a bit because you don’t want to put your backside into someone else’s head,” he said.
“To lead in front on.”
“It’s how we were all taught as kids,” Mooney responded.
“Does the AFL want to discourage the turning of your backside and go in head-first rather is the way they want you to go?” Russell questioned. 
Colby McKercher was subbed out of the game following this first quarter collision with Jeremy Cameron.
North Melbourne was given AFL assistance at the end of last season after another luckless year. 
But even with that help, the Roos remain winless in 2024 – with fears their year could be just as long as the last.
North started 2023 with back-to-back wins in promising signs under new coach Alastair Clarkson.
But Clarkson and the Roos haven’t been able to replicate that start.
“Tough year for the Kangaroos who are yet to have a win this season,” commentator Dwayne Russell said on Fox Footy.
To fully appreciate the chasm between these sides, consider that Bailey Scott has played more AFL games than Brad Close but after today Close will have played in 48 more wins.
“At least they started well last year (with two straight wins). It’s been a tough time.
“Guys like Eddie Ford playing today - 26 games for only one win … makes it tough for development.”
Three-time premiership winner Cameron Mooney forecast another “tough year” at Arden Street.
“It’s going to be a tough year as a key forward for North Melbourne.”
Debutant Tyler Sellers didn’t have a touch until late in the final term as he struggled to get near it, making just one tackle in three quarters of footy. 
Originally published as
en_subtitle: Geelong’s dominant start to the 2024 season reached destructive heights on Sunday as they tore North Melbourne to shreds at home.

title: Ancient Tongan city discovered by Australian researchers using cutting edge technology
subtitle: It could be one of the first ancient cities of its kind to have been discovered but you would never imagine looking at the structure from ground level.
content:
Australian archaeologists have uncovered an ancient city in Tonga using cutting edge technology. 
Lead author, PhD scholar Phillip Parton, from the Australian National University used aerial laser scanners to map archaeological sites on the island of Tongatapu, in Tonga.
It could be one of the first cities of the Pacific. 
The report noted that it was evidence that the first cities in the area were established much earlier than previously thought.
Their findings also point to the fact that Indigenous inhabitants began urbanising their society well before westerners arrived on their shores. 
“Earth structures were being constructed in Tongatapu around AD 300 (300 CE),” Mr Parton said. 
“This is 700 years earlier than previously thought.”
The researchers were able to identify nearly 10,000 mounds that once belonged to ancestors of the island.
“When people think of early cities they usually think of traditional old European cities with compact housing and windy cobblestone streets. This is a very different kind of city,” Mr Parton added.
Another exciting discovery that came out of it was their revelations around urbanisation. 
Mr Parton said traditionally, studying urbanisation in the Pacific was hard due to challenges collecting data, making it difficult to know when it first started happening. 
But aerial laser scanners have changed that.
“We were able to combine high-tech mapping and archaeological fieldwork to understand what was happening in Tongatapu,” he said.
“Urbanisation is not an area that had been investigated much until now. 
“But it shows the contribution of the Pacific to urban science. We can see clues that Tongatapu’s influence spread across the southwest Pacific Ocean between the 13th and 19th centuries.”
According to Mr Parton, the low density low-density urbanisation in Tonga worked perfectly well, but the influence of Europeans changed the way the island built and structured its housing.

title: Big Qantas call as world prepares for attack
subtitle: Qantas has made a major call as the US has reportedly rushed warships into position amid spiralling fears of a major escalation in the Middle East.
content:
The US has reportedly rushed warships into position in a bid to protect Israel and American forces in the Middle East — as fears of a major escalation in the region spiral.
It is understood Israel is preparing itself for a direct attack from Iran within days or even hours.
If that was to happen it would trigger a major military response from the Jewish state and risk a regional war in the Middle East.
Qantas has been forced to redirect its non-stop route from Perth to London to avoid flying over parts of Iran and the Middle East due to concerns over the tensions.
QF9, usually a 17-hour journey direct from Perth to London, will now stop in Singapore to allow the aircraft to carry a full load of passengers and refuel for an alternative path. The return flight, QF10, will still be able to operate as a direct flight on an alternative path due to prevailing winds.
“We’re temporarily adjusting the flight paths for our flights between Perth and London due to the situation in parts of the Middle East,” a Qantas spokesman said.
“We’ll reach out to customers directly if there’s any change to their booking.”
The US military has repositioned two destroyers. One was already in the region and another that was redirected there, the 
American F15-e fighters have also been moved from RAF Lakenheath in the UK to the Middle East, according to military blogger OSINTdefender.
The US will try to to intercept launches at Israel if possible, CNN reports. 
US President Joe Biden saying he expects Iran to attack Israel “sooner than later”.
The chilling warning came after Iran threatened reprisals over a strike in Syria this month that killed two Iranian generals.
As talks for a truce and hostage release dragged on, fears that Iran could soon launch an attack on Israel prompted the United States to announce it was sending reinforcements to the Middle East as a deterrent.
Biden said he expected Iran to strike Israel, but warned it against attacking the US ally in retaliation for the April 1 strike on its Damascus consulate.
Israel said it had strengthened air defences and paused leave for combat units, following a deadly April 1 air strike that destroyed Iran’s consulate building in Damascus.
Iran blamed its arch foe Israel, which has stepped up strikes against Iran-linked targets in Syria since the Gaza war began.
The White House said on Friday that the threat from Iran remained “real”.
Asked what his message was to Iran on striking Israel, Biden said: “Don’t”. 
“We are devoted to the defence of Israel, we will support Israel, we will help defend Israel and Iran will not succeed,” he said. 
A defence official said the Pentagon was “moving additional assets to the region to bolster regional deterrence efforts and increase force protection for US forces.” 
Biden sent the head of US Central Command, General Michael Kurilla, to Israel for urgent talks on the threat from Iran.
After meeting Kurilla on Friday, Defence Minister Yoav Gallant said Israel and the United States stood “shoulder to shoulder”, despite recent differences over the conduct of the war in Gaza.
“Our enemies think that they can pull apart Israel and the United States, but the opposite is true — they are bringing us together and strengthening our ties,” Gallant said.
Washington, which has had no diplomatic relations with Tehran since the aftermath of the 1979 Islamic revolution, also asked its allies to use their influence with Iran to urge restraint, the State Department said.
After calls with his Australian, British and German counterparts Thursday, Foreign Minister Hossein Amir-Abdollahian said: “Iran does not seek to expand the scope of the war.”
But he added that it felt it had no choice but to respond to the deadly attack on its diplomatic mission after the UN Security Council failed to take action.
Lebanon’s Iran-backed militant group Hezbollah said it fired “dozens of Katyusha rockets” at Israeli artillery positions Friday, a bombardment it said was in response to Israeli strikes in the south. 
The Israeli army said approximately 40 launches were identified, some of which were intercepted. “No injuries were reported,” it added.
US stocks were volatile on Friday over the geopolitical tensions as Wall Street investors get skittish. 
Market watchers cited worries about an imminent attack on Israel by Iran.
After spending the entire session in the red, the Dow Jones Industrial Average finished down 1.2 per cent, or 475 points, at 37,983.24.
The broad-based S & P 500 dropped 1.5 per cent to 5,123.41, while the tech-rich Nasdaq Composite Index shed 1.6 per cent to 16,175.09. 
France warned its nationals against travelling to Iran, Israel, Lebanon or the Palestinian territories, after the US embassy in Israel announced it was restricting the movements of its diplomats over security fears.
German airline Lufthansa said its planes would no longer use Iranian airspace as it extended a suspension of flights to and from Tehran.
In their October attack, Hamas militants seized about 250 hostages, 129 of whom remain in Gaza, including 34 the Israeli army says are dead.
The European Union on Friday imposed sanctions on the armed wings of Hamas and Islamic Jihad for “widespread” sexual violence during the October 7 attack. 
The bloc said fighters from the two militant groups — already on the EU’s terrorism blacklist — “committed widespread sexual and gender-based violence in a systematic manner, using it as a weapon of war”.
Washington has ramped up pressure on Netanyahu to increase aid flows to Gaza in the face of UN warnings of imminent famine.
The Israeli army said that an undisclosed number of aid trucks had been allowed to enter Gaza Thursday through a newly opened border crossing into the north of the territory.
“The first food aid trucks entered through the new northern crossing from Israel into Gaza yesterday,” the Israeli defence ministry body that oversees Palestinian civil affairs, COGAT, said.
Despite repeated AFP requests for comment, Israeli authorities did not disclose the exact location of the new crossing, which Israeli media reported to be close to the Zikim kibbutz.
Gallant had trumpeted the new crossing on Wednesday, promising to “flood Gaza with aid”, but on Thursday the UN Security Council said “more should be done”.

title: UFC 300 delivers as Holloway stuns with ‘greatest KO of all time’, Pereira plans ‘unheard of’ move
subtitle: UFC 300 promised so much, with 12 current or former champions headlining the promotion’s historic card – and it most certainly delivered.
content:
UFC 300 promised so much, with 12 current or former champions headlining the promotion’s historic card – and it most certainly delivered.
A main card, which included what Joe Rogan described as the “greatest knockout of all time”, ended with another thunderous finish as Alex Pereira floored Jamahal Hill with a brutal straight left hand.
Referee Herb Dean had initially stepped in to give Pereira time to recover after what looked to be a low blow from Hill, only for the champion to wave him off and then rock the challenger before finishing him off with a relentless ground-and-pound.
With it, Pereira made a statement in his first light heavyweight title defence to cap off what were three incredible fights to close out the card.
“What a killer,” Rogan said of Pereira.
Pereira, who now has eight knockouts to his name, has rapidly risen up the ranks in the UFC since making his debut over two years ago, winning championships in two weight divisions.
So, what possibly could come next? Well, if you take Pereira’s post-fight comments seriously, it could be a shot at interim heavyweight champion Tom Aspinall in Brazil.
“I want to continue defending this belt,” he said.
“I’ve talked a lot about fighting in Brazil if I won this fight. I won this fight. I’m not hurt. Nothing happened. I know there needs to be a lot of promotion behind a fight but I want to have a fight at heavyweight.”
UFC Hall of Famer Daniel Cormier said it would be “nuts” to even watch that fight.
“Even if he attempts to do this it would be unheard of,” Cormier added.
Dana White confirmed earlier in the week that the fight of the night and performance of the night would receive $300,000 bonuses, although Pereira has competition in Max Holloway.
Fighting for ‘The Baddest Motherf***er’ belt, Holloway and Justin Gaethje decided to let loose in the final 10 seconds of their five-round war.
Holloway already looked set to take a unanimous decision victory at that point but the former featherweight champion left no doubt as 
Gaethje had done well to remain in the fight by that point after appearing to break his nose in the opening round, which was the first part of a championship triple-header at UFC 300.
Gaethje landed a leg kick early in the round but it was otherwise Holloway who had the better moments in the first round, mixing up jabs to the body and the head. 
Holloway landed two strong left hands early before getting through with a right that had Gaethje backing up.
The former featherweight champion then ended the round with a bang, landing a spinning kick that appeared to have broken Gaethje’s nose.
“It looks like that nose is destroyed,” Joe Rogan said in commentary.
As if that wasn’t enough, two accidental eye pokes from Holloway in the second round only made matters worse for Gaethje.
“Oh no, that’s the other eye now, oh my goodness,” Rogan added.
Holloway continued to land some heavy shots in the third round, including a big right hand and spinning leg kick, that had Gaethje briefly stumbling.
But Gaethje, despite all the adversity he faced early in the fight, found a way to drop Holloway in the fourth after landing a right hand on the temple as the pair traded blows on the inside.
With it, Gaethje scored the first knockdown of Holloway’s career and finished the round with plenty of momentum, although the former champion took it right back in the fifth.
Holloway rocked Gaethje with a lethal combination and then backed him up against the cage, even mixing in a spinning kick at one point.
But still Gaethje stood tall, right up until the final second, when Holloway sent him crumbling to the canvas in emphatic fashion.
“An absolute stunner,” Rogan said.
“... That is the greatest knockout of all time, with so many people counting him out.”
Now, having once looked out of the featherweight title picture following a third loss to Alexander Volkanovski, Holloway is firmly back in the frame for a shot at champion Ilia Topuria. 
The unbelievable moments kept coming as Yan Xiaonan was just saved by the bell in the opening round of her unanimous decision defeat to champion Zhang Weili in their women’s strawweight fight.
Yan looked to be in serious trouble as Zhang locked in an incredibly tight rear-naked choke with 15 seconds left in the first round.
“This is incredible,” Rogan said in commentary as Yan appeared to be on the brink of passing out, with the bell sounded as the challenger stumbled to her feet, hardly looking in any position to continue.
“She was out,” Rogan added.
Yan continued to prove her toughness in the second round too as Zhang immediately went to take the fight to the ground once more, doing so successfully and brutally unleashing ground-and-pound punches.
Once again it looked like the fight was on the brink of being finished but Yan was able to survive the onslaught before finding herself in peril again, this time as Zhang locked in a tight arm triangle choke.
Yan though once again was able to eventually escape and then opened the third round by dropping the champion and went on to score two takedowns to flip the fight on its head.
The challenger carried that dominance in the fourth too, dropping Zhang again, this time with a perfect counter as she followed up a right hand with a perfectly timed left hook.
Zhang responded by scoring a takedown and went back to the ground-and-pound, maintaining back control for a good few minutes while inflicting enough damage to leave her challenger’s eye damaged.
The champion was able to take Yan to the mat again in the fifth round, doing enough to defend her belt.
Earlier, Bo Nickal opened the main card with his third straight submission victory to improve to 3-0 in the promotion, although he wasn’t too happy with his performance.
After submitting Cody Brundage with a second-round rear-naked choke, Nickal booed himself and gave two thumbs down, later telling Joe Rogan he was “a little bit embarrassed” with his performance.
“I expect to go out and completely dominate and shut that guy out,” Nickal said.
“... But at the same time I am also grateful I got to get past the first round... I have that experience under my belt now.”
Brundage was aggressive early, pressuring Nickal to try make his undefeated rival uncomfortable and even flashing an unsuccessful flying knee and spinning back fist in the opening minute.
It was an energetic opening from Brundage but that quickly came to an end as Nickal took him to the ground and maintained back control for the majority of the opening round.
Nickal initially went for the rear-naked choke but wasn’t able to get his arm under the neck of Brundage and that remained the case for the rest of the round, with the veteran never really looking in trouble.
Before Sunday Nickal’s longest fight in this MMA career had been two minutes and 54 seconds, although being taken into the deep waters wasn’t necessarily going to be such a bad thing for his development.
Although again Brundage found himself on the ground early in the second round and while he was surviving once more, Nickal was still doing enough to cruise his way to victory unless something dramatically changed. 
Unfortunately for Brundage that was not the case as Nickal eventually was able to get the submission and move to 6-0 in the process.
Elsewhere, Arman Tsarukyan outlasted current No. 1 ranked contender Charles Oliveira in an epic lightweight bout by split decision (29-28 x2, 28-29).
Oliveira, one of the best submission artists in the UFC’s history, looked to have secured an early finish in the opening round when he locked in a deep guillotine.
Tsarukyan though kept composed and was able to somehow escape before then rolling Oliveira over and getting on top in the guard of the Brazilian, flipping the script after he found himself in danger early in the round. 
There was some controversy too in the first round as Oliveira was warned over an illegal strike off the ground which Daniel Cormier said in commentary could have caused much more damage than it did in the end.
“Luckily it hit his shoulder first because if that went straight to the face he may have gone down,” Cormier said. 
Oliveira was not deducted points for the move as the fight then went to the second round, with the former lightweight champion opening the second by landing a big right hand.
Tsarukyan though ate it right up and then later fired a brutal axe kick before later landing a takedown as the fight once again became an intriguing ground battle.
Oliveira was largely able to defend himself before Tsarukyan landed some brutal elbows that spilt his rival open, although the Brazilian lasted until the bell as the fight went to a third and final round.
There was late drama as Oliveira locked in a D’arce choke with 20 seconds left but once again Tsaruykan remained unfazed as he waited it out, surviving to take the victory.
“I want to fight for the title next,” Tsaruykan said.
Elsewhere, the preliminary card opened up with one of the UFC’s fast-rising stars and a debutant making big statements.
First up, Diego Lopes made it three finishes in a row as he knocked out Sodiq Yussuf in the opening round of their featherweight bout.
Then, two-time Olympic gold medal winning judoka Kayla Harrison celebrated her UFC debut in style as she submitted former women’s bantamweight champion and No. 5 ranked contender Holly Holm in the second round of their fight.
Harrison told Rogan in her post-fight interview that she wants a title shot against Raquel Pennington next, declaring she will be a UFC champion by the end of the year.
“That’s what I came over here for. One thing and one thing only,” she added.
Meanwhile, Lopes called out Movsar Evloev, who he lost to in his UFC debut last year after taking the fight on short notice, but also welcomed a shot at the featherweight champion.
“If he doesn’t want to fight with me then I’ll go to Spain for Ilia Topuria,” Lopes said.
Later fights on the preliminary card saw former bantamweight champion Aljamain Sterling score a dominant victory in his UFC debut at 145 pounds, taking care of business against featherweight contender Calvin Kattar in a unanimous decision win.
The fight failed to deliver any real fireworks, although the same couldn’t be said for Jiri Prochazka’s TKO victory over Aleksandar Rakic.
The former UFC light heavyweight champion and No. 2 ranked contender looked compromised in the opening round after struggling to check constant leg kicks from Rakic, although Prochazka only increased his aggression in the second to hunt the finish.
Prochazka got just that, blitzing Rakic to force Herb Dean to step in and call an end to the fight.
Speaking after the victory, Prochazka said he wants a shot at the win of the Pereira vs Hill main event.
Prochazka was stopped by Pereira in the second round of their bout for the then vacant UFC light heavyweight title in November last year.
Elsewhere, Jalin Turner suffered a second-round TKO defeat against Renato Moicano after a humiliating blunder. 
Turner looked to be on top early, hurting Moicano twice with his pinpoint striking before appearing to put his rival on the brink of defeat with a brutal straight left hand.
It sent Moicano crashing to the canvas, seemingly gifting Turner the win should the American had immediately capitalised on the opportunity to finish him via ground and pound.
Instead, Turner appeared to believe he had scored a walk-off knockout and it proved a big mistake as Moicano recovered to get back on his feet and continue the fight. 
“He thought he had him and he was walking off, which is very interesting that he did that,” Joe Rogan said in commentary.
“That’s a problem. You try to create moments when in reality he should have tried to finish it,” added Daniel Cormier.
JALIN TURNER KNOCKS DOWM MOICANO AND THINKS THAT ITS OVER??? 
Moicano eventually got the finish as he showed Turner how to do it the right way, sealing the victory with a vicious ground and pound after taking him to the canvas.
“Nobody can hurt me,” Moicano said. 
“I’m ‘Money f******* Moicano. I’m not going to stop until I have the belt. UFC, you need to respect me, I am the best in the business.”
Earlier, the historic card kicked off in bloody fashion as veteran lightweight Bobby Green outlasted Jim Miller in a brutal three-round war on Sunday. 
Green dominated the fight on the way to a unanimous decision victory, with the scorecards reading 30-27, 30-25 and 29-26 and almost got the finish in the third round. 
On that occasion he knocked Miller to the canvas but the 40-year-old just refused to give in, making it all the way to the final bell.
It was a long-awaited fight between the pair after a knee injury saw their first scheduled bout in 2014 fall through, while then seven years later Green was set to step in at short-notice to fight Miller but collapsed after the weigh-in.
Then in 2022, when the pair were finally set to face off in the octagon, Green failed a drug test. 
Green said Miller’s comments earlier in the week suggesting he was to blame for the cancellation of their multiple fights was a source of motivation for him on Sunday.
“It wasn’t nothing personal for me... I’m just going to hit him a lot. For everything you were saying, I’m going to hit you a lot,” Green told the UFC.
“He was cut up, he was busted up, he was bloody and I told you, ‘I’m going to hit you a lot’. I think I got the record right now for the most amount of hits in my division and I keep climbing it and climbing it.”
With the win at UFC 300, Green rebounded from a savage knockout loss to Jalin Turner in December and now turns his attention towards a potential fight with Paddy Pimblett.
Why? 
“Paddy said my name and anybody who has ever said my name, I will find you... you say my name and I shall appear,” Green said.
“In his backyard, with his people, with all the opportunity in his favour and watch what I do.”
Alex Pereira (c) def. Jamahal Hill via KO in Round 1 — for light heavyweight title
Weili Zhang (c) def. Yan Xiaonan via UD (49-45 x3) — for women’s strawweight title
Max Holloway def. Justin Gaethje (c) via knockout in Round 5 — for BMF title
Arman Tasrukyan def. Charles Oliveira via SD (29-28 x2, 28-29) — lightweight
Bo Nickal def. Cody Brundage via submission in Round 2 — middleweight
Jiri Prochazka def. Aleksandar Rakic via TKO in Round 2 — light heavyweight
Aljamain Sterling def. Calvin Kattar via UD (30-27 x3) — featherweight
Kayla Harrison def. Holly Holm via submission in Round 2 — women’s bantamweight
Diego Lopes def. Sodiq Yusuff via TKO in Round 1 — featherweight
Renato Moicano def. Jalin Turner via TKO in Round 2
Jessica Andrade def. Mariana Rodriguez via SD (29-28 x 2, 28-29)
Bobby Green def. Jim Miller via UD (30-27, 29-26, 30-25)
Deiveson Figueiredo def. Cody Garbrandt via submission in Round 2
Originally published as
en_title: UFC 300 delivers as Holloway stuns with ‘greatest KO of all time’, Pereira plans ‘unheard of’ move
en_content:
UFC 300 promised so much, with 12 current or former champions headlining the promotion’s historic card – and it most certainly delivered.
A main card, which included what Joe Rogan described as the “greatest knockout of all time”, ended with another thunderous finish as Alex Pereira floored Jamahal Hill with a brutal straight left hand.
Referee Herb Dean had initially stepped in to give Pereira time to recover after what looked to be a low blow from Hill, only for the champion to wave him off and then rock the challenger before finishing him off with a relentless ground-and-pound.
With it, Pereira made a statement in his first light heavyweight title defence to cap off what were three incredible fights to close out the card.
“What a killer,” Rogan said of Pereira.
Pereira, who now has eight knockouts to his name, has rapidly risen up the ranks in the UFC since making his debut over two years ago, winning championships in two weight divisions.
So, what possibly could come next? Well, if you take Pereira’s post-fight comments seriously, it could be a shot at interim heavyweight champion Tom Aspinall in Brazil.
“I want to continue defending this belt,” he said.
“I’ve talked a lot about fighting in Brazil if I won this fight. I won this fight. I’m not hurt. Nothing happened. I know there needs to be a lot of promotion behind a fight but I want to have a fight at heavyweight.”
UFC Hall of Famer Daniel Cormier said it would be “nuts” to even watch that fight.
“Even if he attempts to do this it would be unheard of,” Cormier added.
Dana White confirmed earlier in the week that the fight of the night and performance of the night would receive $300,000 bonuses, although Pereira has competition in Max Holloway.
Fighting for ‘The Baddest Motherf***er’ belt, Holloway and Justin Gaethje decided to let loose in the final 10 seconds of their five-round war.
Holloway already looked set to take a unanimous decision victory at that point but the former featherweight champion left no doubt as 
Gaethje had done well to remain in the fight by that point after appearing to break his nose in the opening round, which was the first part of a championship triple-header at UFC 300.
Gaethje landed a leg kick early in the round but it was otherwise Holloway who had the better moments in the first round, mixing up jabs to the body and the head. 
Holloway landed two strong left hands early before getting through with a right that had Gaethje backing up.
The former featherweight champion then ended the round with a bang, landing a spinning kick that appeared to have broken Gaethje’s nose.
“It looks like that nose is destroyed,” Joe Rogan said in commentary.
As if that wasn’t enough, two accidental eye pokes from Holloway in the second round only made matters worse for Gaethje.
“Oh no, that’s the other eye now, oh my goodness,” Rogan added.
Holloway continued to land some heavy shots in the third round, including a big right hand and spinning leg kick, that had Gaethje briefly stumbling.
But Gaethje, despite all the adversity he faced early in the fight, found a way to drop Holloway in the fourth after landing a right hand on the temple as the pair traded blows on the inside.
With it, Gaethje scored the first knockdown of Holloway’s career and finished the round with plenty of momentum, although the former champion took it right back in the fifth.
Holloway rocked Gaethje with a lethal combination and then backed him up against the cage, even mixing in a spinning kick at one point.
But still Gaethje stood tall, right up until the final second, when Holloway sent him crumbling to the canvas in emphatic fashion.
“An absolute stunner,” Rogan said.
“... That is the greatest knockout of all time, with so many people counting him out.”
Now, having once looked out of the featherweight title picture following a third loss to Alexander Volkanovski, Holloway is firmly back in the frame for a shot at champion Ilia Topuria. 
The unbelievable moments kept coming as Yan Xiaonan was just saved by the bell in the opening round of her unanimous decision defeat to champion Zhang Weili in their women’s strawweight fight.
Yan looked to be in serious trouble as Zhang locked in an incredibly tight rear-naked choke with 15 seconds left in the first round.
“This is incredible,” Rogan said in commentary as Yan appeared to be on the brink of passing out, with the bell sounded as the challenger stumbled to her feet, hardly looking in any position to continue.
“She was out,” Rogan added.
Yan continued to prove her toughness in the second round too as Zhang immediately went to take the fight to the ground once more, doing so successfully and brutally unleashing ground-and-pound punches.
Once again it looked like the fight was on the brink of being finished but Yan was able to survive the onslaught before finding herself in peril again, this time as Zhang locked in a tight arm triangle choke.
Yan though once again was able to eventually escape and then opened the third round by dropping the champion and went on to score two takedowns to flip the fight on its head.
The challenger carried that dominance in the fourth too, dropping Zhang again, this time with a perfect counter as she followed up a right hand with a perfectly timed left hook.
Zhang responded by scoring a takedown and went back to the ground-and-pound, maintaining back control for a good few minutes while inflicting enough damage to leave her challenger’s eye damaged.
The champion was able to take Yan to the mat again in the fifth round, doing enough to defend her belt.
Earlier, Bo Nickal opened the main card with his third straight submission victory to improve to 3-0 in the promotion, although he wasn’t too happy with his performance.
After submitting Cody Brundage with a second-round rear-naked choke, Nickal booed himself and gave two thumbs down, later telling Joe Rogan he was “a little bit embarrassed” with his performance.
“I expect to go out and completely dominate and shut that guy out,” Nickal said.
“... But at the same time I am also grateful I got to get past the first round... I have that experience under my belt now.”
Brundage was aggressive early, pressuring Nickal to try make his undefeated rival uncomfortable and even flashing an unsuccessful flying knee and spinning back fist in the opening minute.
It was an energetic opening from Brundage but that quickly came to an end as Nickal took him to the ground and maintained back control for the majority of the opening round.
Nickal initially went for the rear-naked choke but wasn’t able to get his arm under the neck of Brundage and that remained the case for the rest of the round, with the veteran never really looking in trouble.
Before Sunday Nickal’s longest fight in this MMA career had been two minutes and 54 seconds, although being taken into the deep waters wasn’t necessarily going to be such a bad thing for his development.
Although again Brundage found himself on the ground early in the second round and while he was surviving once more, Nickal was still doing enough to cruise his way to victory unless something dramatically changed. 
Unfortunately for Brundage that was not the case as Nickal eventually was able to get the submission and move to 6-0 in the process.
Elsewhere, Arman Tsarukyan outlasted current No. 1 ranked contender Charles Oliveira in an epic lightweight bout by split decision (29-28 x2, 28-29).
Oliveira, one of the best submission artists in the UFC’s history, looked to have secured an early finish in the opening round when he locked in a deep guillotine.
Tsarukyan though kept composed and was able to somehow escape before then rolling Oliveira over and getting on top in the guard of the Brazilian, flipping the script after he found himself in danger early in the round. 
There was some controversy too in the first round as Oliveira was warned over an illegal strike off the ground which Daniel Cormier said in commentary could have caused much more damage than it did in the end.
“Luckily it hit his shoulder first because if that went straight to the face he may have gone down,” Cormier said. 
Oliveira was not deducted points for the move as the fight then went to the second round, with the former lightweight champion opening the second by landing a big right hand.
Tsarukyan though ate it right up and then later fired a brutal axe kick before later landing a takedown as the fight once again became an intriguing ground battle.
Oliveira was largely able to defend himself before Tsarukyan landed some brutal elbows that spilt his rival open, although the Brazilian lasted until the bell as the fight went to a third and final round.
There was late drama as Oliveira locked in a D’arce choke with 20 seconds left but once again Tsaruykan remained unfazed as he waited it out, surviving to take the victory.
“I want to fight for the title next,” Tsaruykan said.
Elsewhere, the preliminary card opened up with one of the UFC’s fast-rising stars and a debutant making big statements.
First up, Diego Lopes made it three finishes in a row as he knocked out Sodiq Yussuf in the opening round of their featherweight bout.
Then, two-time Olympic gold medal winning judoka Kayla Harrison celebrated her UFC debut in style as she submitted former women’s bantamweight champion and No. 5 ranked contender Holly Holm in the second round of their fight.
Harrison told Rogan in her post-fight interview that she wants a title shot against Raquel Pennington next, declaring she will be a UFC champion by the end of the year.
“That’s what I came over here for. One thing and one thing only,” she added.
Meanwhile, Lopes called out Movsar Evloev, who he lost to in his UFC debut last year after taking the fight on short notice, but also welcomed a shot at the featherweight champion.
“If he doesn’t want to fight with me then I’ll go to Spain for Ilia Topuria,” Lopes said.
Later fights on the preliminary card saw former bantamweight champion Aljamain Sterling score a dominant victory in his UFC debut at 145 pounds, taking care of business against featherweight contender Calvin Kattar in a unanimous decision win.
The fight failed to deliver any real fireworks, although the same couldn’t be said for Jiri Prochazka’s TKO victory over Aleksandar Rakic.
The former UFC light heavyweight champion and No. 2 ranked contender looked compromised in the opening round after struggling to check constant leg kicks from Rakic, although Prochazka only increased his aggression in the second to hunt the finish.
Prochazka got just that, blitzing Rakic to force Herb Dean to step in and call an end to the fight.
Speaking after the victory, Prochazka said he wants a shot at the win of the Pereira vs Hill main event.
Prochazka was stopped by Pereira in the second round of their bout for the then vacant UFC light heavyweight title in November last year.
Elsewhere, Jalin Turner suffered a second-round TKO defeat against Renato Moicano after a humiliating blunder. 
Turner looked to be on top early, hurting Moicano twice with his pinpoint striking before appearing to put his rival on the brink of defeat with a brutal straight left hand.
It sent Moicano crashing to the canvas, seemingly gifting Turner the win should the American had immediately capitalised on the opportunity to finish him via ground and pound.
Instead, Turner appeared to believe he had scored a walk-off knockout and it proved a big mistake as Moicano recovered to get back on his feet and continue the fight. 
“He thought he had him and he was walking off, which is very interesting that he did that,” Joe Rogan said in commentary.
“That’s a problem. You try to create moments when in reality he should have tried to finish it,” added Daniel Cormier.
JALIN TURNER KNOCKS DOWM MOICANO AND THINKS THAT ITS OVER??? 
Moicano eventually got the finish as he showed Turner how to do it the right way, sealing the victory with a vicious ground and pound after taking him to the canvas.
“Nobody can hurt me,” Moicano said. 
“I’m ‘Money f******* Moicano. I’m not going to stop until I have the belt. UFC, you need to respect me, I am the best in the business.”
Earlier, the historic card kicked off in bloody fashion as veteran lightweight Bobby Green outlasted Jim Miller in a brutal three-round war on Sunday. 
Green dominated the fight on the way to a unanimous decision victory, with the scorecards reading 30-27, 30-25 and 29-26 and almost got the finish in the third round. 
On that occasion he knocked Miller to the canvas but the 40-year-old just refused to give in, making it all the way to the final bell.
It was a long-awaited fight between the pair after a knee injury saw their first scheduled bout in 2014 fall through, while then seven years later Green was set to step in at short-notice to fight Miller but collapsed after the weigh-in.
Then in 2022, when the pair were finally set to face off in the octagon, Green failed a drug test. 
Green said Miller’s comments earlier in the week suggesting he was to blame for the cancellation of their multiple fights was a source of motivation for him on Sunday.
“It wasn’t nothing personal for me... I’m just going to hit him a lot. For everything you were saying, I’m going to hit you a lot,” Green told the UFC.
“He was cut up, he was busted up, he was bloody and I told you, ‘I’m going to hit you a lot’. I think I got the record right now for the most amount of hits in my division and I keep climbing it and climbing it.”
With the win at UFC 300, Green rebounded from a savage knockout loss to Jalin Turner in December and now turns his attention towards a potential fight with Paddy Pimblett.
Why? 
“Paddy said my name and anybody who has ever said my name, I will find you... you say my name and I shall appear,” Green said.
“In his backyard, with his people, with all the opportunity in his favour and watch what I do.”
Alex Pereira (c) def. Jamahal Hill via KO in Round 1 — for light heavyweight title
Weili Zhang (c) def. Yan Xiaonan via UD (49-45 x3) — for women’s strawweight title
Max Holloway def. Justin Gaethje (c) via knockout in Round 5 — for BMF title
Arman Tasrukyan def. Charles Oliveira via SD (29-28 x2, 28-29) — lightweight
Bo Nickal def. Cody Brundage via submission in Round 2 — middleweight
Jiri Prochazka def. Aleksandar Rakic via TKO in Round 2 — light heavyweight
Aljamain Sterling def. Calvin Kattar via UD (30-27 x3) — featherweight
Kayla Harrison def. Holly Holm via submission in Round 2 — women’s bantamweight
Diego Lopes def. Sodiq Yusuff via TKO in Round 1 — featherweight
Renato Moicano def. Jalin Turner via TKO in Round 2
Jessica Andrade def. Mariana Rodriguez via SD (29-28 x 2, 28-29)
Bobby Green def. Jim Miller via UD (30-27, 29-26, 30-25)
Deiveson Figueiredo def. Cody Garbrandt via submission in Round 2
Originally published as
en_subtitle: UFC 300 promised so much, with 12 current or former champions headlining the promotion’s historic card – and it most certainly delivered.

title: Woman charged after Australian bulldog allegedly thrown off cliff near Wollongong
subtitle: A woman has been charged with animal cruelty offences after allegedly throwing a pet dog off a cliff.
content:
A woman has been charged after allegedly throwing a dog off a cliff south of Sydney, following a lengthy investigation by police.
The 32-year-old was arrested at a Horsley home in Wollongong on Friday morning and bailed after investigators charged her with animal cruelty offences after the animal was euthanised. 
The move followed a more than year-long investigation after a bushwalker heard a dog yelping near Clive Bissell Dr at Mount Ousley in January last year. 
Searching around, he discovered a kennel tipped upside down at the base of an escarpment with an injured Australian Bulldog inside about 8.30am. 
The find prompted a rescue operation involving police and SES volunteers, but the dog was later euthanised due to his poor condition and injuries. 
Police will allege the kennel was thrown from the cliff overnight on January 22, 2023.
Investigators began a forensic examination of the kennel and arrested the woman following a “extensive investigation”, a NSW Police spokesman said. 
She is expected to appear in the Wollongong Local Court in May.
en_title: Woman charged after Australian bulldog allegedly thrown off cliff near Wollongong

title: Families scarred, lives lost: Victims of the Bondi Junction attack remembered
subtitle: The sixth victim killed at Bondi Junction on Saturday has now been identified. Read more about the people whose lives have been cut short.
content:
The sixth victim of Saturday’s rampage has now been identified. 
Yixuan Cheng, a Chinese national who was studying in Australia, is the sixth fatality in the stabbing attack to be named.
Police were on Sunday trying to contact her family overseas.
A fuller picture of the lives taken at Bondi Junction on Saturday is emerging.
Joel Cauchi killed five women and one man in the busy shopping centre and put an additional 12 people, including a baby, in hospital.
The six people Cauchi killed have now been identified as of Monday morning. They are: Yixuan Cheng 27, Dawn Singleton, 25, Faraz Ahmed Tahir, 30, Ashlee Good, 38, Jade Young, 47, and Pikria Darchia, 55.
Hero police officer Amy Scott shot Cauchi dead at the scene.
Dawn Singleton was engaged to marry a NSW police officer later this year, The Daily Telegraph reports.
She is the daughter of highly successful Australian businessman John Singleton and lawyer Julie Martin.
Security guard and former refugee Faraz Ahmed Tahir is the lone male victim to have been killed.
He was at work when he was attacked and succumbed to his injuries.
A member of the Ahmadiyya Muslim Community of Australia, the community said in a statement that Mr Tahir was known for his “unwavering dedication and kindness”. 
Mr Tahir arrived in Australia about a year ago from Sri Lanka via the United Nations High Commissioner for Refugees from Pakistan, where his parents still live.
The Ahmadiyya community is a sect of Islam, but Ahmadis face persecution for their religion in Muslim-majority countries like Algeria, Malaysia and Pakistan.
Under the Pakistani constitution, the community is declared non-Muslims, has been subject to violent attacks, and is not allowed to vote due to their religious beliefs.
“Faraz was not only a valued member of the Ahmadiyya Muslim Community of Australia but also actively contributed to the charitable endeavours of the Ahmadiyya Muslim Youth organisation,” the Australian religious group’s statement said.
Ashlee Good and her nine-month-old daughter Harriet were both stabbed during the attack.
Ms Good died of her injuries but not before she handed her baby to two men to try to save the infant.
Ms Good’s family have thanked the public for the messages of support and the two men, saying the nine-month-old is recovering after surgery. 
“After hours of surgery yesterday our baby is currently doing well,” the family said in a statement.
The family was grateful for the expert care at Sydney Children’s Hospital.
“To the two men who held and cared for our baby when Ashlee could not – words cannot express our gratitude.
“We are struggling to come to terms with what has occurred. We would appreciate peace and privacy while we work through this as a family,” the statement reads.
Bellevue Hill woman and architect Jade Young, 47, has been confirmed as one of the six people who died on Saturday.
The mother-of-two was a beloved member of the Bronte Surf Life Saving Club and long-time employee at a Double Bay architect firm. 
In response to the tragedy, the eastern Sydney surf club issued a Facebook post cancelling their weekly Sunday Sips event. 
“This is and will be a difficult time for the community, and we encourage you to seek support from family, friends, fellow club members or to speak to your GP to assist you in processing this tragedy,” the post read. 
“We are here to support the community, and our heartfelt condolences go out to everyone affected.”
The 55-year-old Bondi Junction woman was murdered in the rampage. 
Her family has requested privacy at this time.
en_title: Families scarred, lives lost: Victims of the Bondi Junction attack remembered

title: Queensland miner reveals $300k salary
subtitle: An Aussie in thongs has candidly admitted how much money he makes and it is yet again proof that most of us are in the wrong job.
content:
A miner has candidly admitted to social media how much money he makes and it is yet again proof that most of us are in the wrong job. 
The self-employed FIFO worker revealed he makes an eye-watering $300,000 a year.
He was bailed up in Brisbane’s city centre for a promotional video from the Getahead team, which operates an app like Tinder but for job searching.
“What do you do and how much do you make?” the Getahead team member asks. 
“Underground coal miner,” the man replied, who was dressed casually wearing thongs and shorts. “I work for myself so nearly 300.” 
The unnamed man revealed he comes from a long line of miners which is how he fell into this kind of work. 
“All my family’s always just done it so been doing it since I was 17,” he said. 
As the interview on the Brisbane street continued, he was asked what kind of qualifications he needed for the role. 
“Anyone can do it,” he said. 
Miners need to complete a Standard 11, a mandatory training module about safety in the mines. 
However, while anyone can do it, not just anyone is cut out for it, he pointed out. 
“I don’t get scared but there are other people that have come down for the first time and wanted to get straight back out because they couldn’t handle it,” he said, adding that sometimes he can be working up to one kilometre underground. 
“It is a dangerous job obviously.”
The video was posted on TikTok and soon comments were flowing in thick and fast. 
One of the sticking points for viewers was what he was wearing. 
Dressed in thongs and shorts, and with a five o’clock shadow, he did cut a rather casual figure. 
“$300k per year with a $20 fit from Kmart,” one person noted. 
The average salary of an Australian miner is $150,000, according to job hiring platform Talent.
Data released at the end of last year 
The highest median personal incomes tended to be in mining towns, where workers often earned twice as much as the typical Aussie.
While these mining towns took out positions one to six, the final places to round out the top 10 were suburbs in Sydney’s north or east.
Those towns were:
Ashburton, WA – $105,655
Roxby Downs, SA – $103,653
Peppermint Grove, WA – $96,684
Port Hedland, WA – $93,230
Weipa, Queensland – $91,494
East Pilbara, WA – $89,562
Woollahra, NSW – $84,255
North Sydney, NSW – $82,706
Mosman, NSW – $78,408
Lane Cove, NSW – $78,323
